mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, compressing equity valuations and generating negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >55% of rate hike periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden increase in the unemployment rate above 4.5 percent signals rapid labor market deterioration and elevated recession probability, triggering immediate negative equity returns as investors de-risk.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances when UNRATE crosses the 4.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand contraction, reducing corporate revenue expectations and driving negative equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative returns with a Sharpe ratio above 0.2 over the 60-day horizon in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 2 percent year-over-year during a supply-constrained regime signals capacity bottlenecks and persistent inflationary pressure, prompting expectations of restrictive policy and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth above 2% correlates with positive 20-day returns in >60% of cases during identified supply bottleneck periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index greater than 0.4 percent signals accelerating inflation, triggering immediate negative equity returns as markets price in aggressive future monetary tightening.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in >50% of instances following a CPIAUCSL month-over-month increase exceeding 0.4%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls above 150,000 supports robust consumer spending and corporate revenue projections, driving positive equity returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth above 150,000 correlates with negative 60-day returns in >55% of cases across rolling validation windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence where quarterly GDP growth decelerates while monthly retail sales accelerate indicates a structural shift toward consumer-driven demand that temporarily boosts equity valuations before a broader economic correction.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-RSAFS divergence signal yields a negative information coefficient over rolling 2-year windows or fails to outperform a buy-and-hold baseline.

mechanism_narrative: During a recessionary regime, a simultaneous decline in industrial production and rise in the unemployment rate confirms deep economic contraction, leading to negative short-term equity returns as earnings forecasts are downgraded.
failure_condition: Signal fails to predict negative returns during NBER-defined recessions with an accuracy rate below 45% in out-of-sample testing.
